<content>1: package planstate
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"os"
6: 	"path/filepath"
7: 	"sort"
8: 	"strings"
9: 	"time"
10: 
11: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/planstore"
12: )
13: 
14: type Status string
15: 
16: const (
17: 	StatusReady     Status = "ready"
18: 	StatusDone      Status = "done"
19: 	StatusReviewing Status = "reviewing"
20: 	StatusCancelled Status = "cancelled"
21: 
22: 	// Lifecycle-stage statuses — canonical names used by the FSM.
23: 	StatusPlanning     Status = "planning"
24: 	StatusImplementing Status = "implementing"
25: )
26: 
27: type PlanEntry struct {
28: 	Status      Status    `json:"status"`
29: 	Description string    `json:"description,omitempty"`
30: 	Branch      string    `json:"branch,omitempty"`
31: 	Topic       string    `json:"topic,omitempty"`
32: 	CreatedAt   time.Time `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
33: 	Implemented string    `json:"implemented,omitempty"`
34: }
35: 
36: type TopicEntry struct {
37: 	CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
38: }
39: 
40: type PlanState struct {
41: 	Dir          string
42: 	Plans        map[string]PlanEntry
43: 	TopicEntries map[string]TopicEntry
44: 	store        planstore.Store // always non-nil
45: 	project      string          // project name used with the store
46: }
47: 
48: type PlanInfo struct {
49: 	Filename    string
50: 	Status      Status
51: 	Description string
52: 	Branch      string
53: 	Topic       string
54: 	CreatedAt   time.Time
55: }
56: 
57: type TopicInfo struct {
58: 	Name      string
59: 	CreatedAt time.Time
60: }
61: 
62: // Load creates a PlanState backed by the given store. Plans and TopicEntries are
63: // populated from the store. dir is retained for file operations (e.g. Rename moves
64: // the .md file on disk). The store is always required — there is no JSON fallback.
65: func Load(store planstore.Store, project, dir string) (*PlanState, error) {
66: 	plans, err := store.List(project)
67: 	if err != nil {
68: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
69: 	}
70: 
71: 	topics, err := store.ListTopics(project)
72: 	if err != nil {
73: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
74: 	}
75: 
76: 	ps := &PlanState{
77: 		Dir:          dir,
78: 		Plans:        make(map[string]PlanEntry, len(plans)),
79: 		TopicEntries: make(map[string]TopicEntry, len(topics)),
80: 		store:        store,
81: 		project:      project,
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	for _, e := range plans {
85: 		ps.Plans[e.Filename] = PlanEntry{
86: 			Status:      Status(e.Status),
87: 			Description: e.Description,
88: 			Branch:      e.Branch,
89: 			Topic:       e.Topic,
90: 			CreatedAt:   e.CreatedAt,
91: 			Implemented: e.Implemented,
92: 		}
93: 	}
94: 
95: 	for _, t := range topics {
96: 		ps.TopicEntries[t.Name] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: t.CreatedAt}
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	return ps, nil
100: }
101: 
102: // Topics returns all topic entries sorted by name.
103: func (ps *PlanState) Topics() []TopicInfo {
104: 	// Discover topics from both TopicEntries and plan topic fields.
105: 	seen := make(map[string]TopicInfo)
106: 	for name, entry := range ps.TopicEntries {
107: 		seen[name] = TopicInfo{Name: name, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt}
108: 	}
109: 	for _, entry := range ps.Plans {
110: 		if entry.Topic != "" {
111: 			if _, ok := seen[entry.Topic]; !ok {
112: 				seen[entry.Topic] = TopicInfo{Name: entry.Topic}
113: 			}
114: 		}
115: 	}
116: 	result := make([]TopicInfo, 0, len(seen))
117: 	for _, info := range seen {
118: 		result = append(result, info)
119: 	}
120: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
121: 		return result[i].Name < result[j].Name
122: 	})
123: 	return result
124: }
125: 
126: // PlansByTopic returns all plans in the given topic, sorted by filename.
127: func (ps *PlanState) PlansByTopic(topic string) []PlanInfo {
128: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
129: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
130: 		if entry.Topic == topic {
131: 			result = append(result, PlanInfo{
132: 				Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
133: 				Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
134: 				Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
135: 			})
136: 		}
137: 	}
138: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
139: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
140: 	})
141: 	return result
142: }
143: 
144: // UngroupedPlans returns all active plans with no topic, sorted by filename.
145: func (ps *PlanState) UngroupedPlans() []PlanInfo {
146: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
147: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
148: 		if entry.Status == StatusDone || entry.Status == StatusCancelled {
149: 			continue
150: 		}
151: 		if entry.Topic == "" {
152: 			result = append(result, PlanInfo{
153: 				Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
154: 				Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
155: 				CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
156: 			})
157: 		}
158: 	}
159: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
160: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
161: 	})
162: 	return result
163: }
164: 
165: // hasTopicEntry returns true if the given topic name exists in TopicEntries.
166: func (ps *PlanState) hasTopicEntry(topic string) bool {
167: 	if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
168: 		return false
169: 	}
170: 	_, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]
171: 	return exists
172: }
173: 
174: // HasRunningCoderInTopic checks if any plan in the given topic (other than
175: // excludePlan) has status StatusImplementing. Returns the conflicting plan filename.
176: func (ps *PlanState) HasRunningCoderInTopic(topic, excludePlan string) (bool, string) {
177: 	if topic == "" {
178: 		return false, ""
179: 	}
180: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
181: 		if filename == excludePlan {
182: 			continue
183: 		}
184: 		if entry.Topic == topic && entry.Status == StatusImplementing {
185: 			return true, filename
186: 		}
187: 	}
188: 	return false, ""
189: }
190: 
191: // Unfinished returns plans that are not done or cancelled, sorted by filename.
192: func (ps *PlanState) Unfinished() []PlanInfo {
193: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0, len(ps.Plans))
194: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
195: 		if entry.Status == StatusDone || entry.Status == StatusCancelled {
196: 			continue
197: 		}
198: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
199: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
200: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
201: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
202: 		})
203: 	}
204: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
205: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
206: 	})
207: 	return result
208: }
209: 
210: // Finished returns plans that are done, sorted by creation time (newest first).
211: func (ps *PlanState) Finished() []PlanInfo {
212: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
213: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
214: 		if entry.Status != StatusDone {
215: 			continue
216: 		}
217: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
218: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
219: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
220: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
221: 		})
222: 	}
223: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
224: 		if !result[i].CreatedAt.Equal(result[j].CreatedAt) {
225: 			return result[i].CreatedAt.After(result[j].CreatedAt)
226: 		}
227: 		return result[i].Filename > result[j].Filename
228: 	})
229: 	return result
230: }
231: 
232: // Cancelled returns all cancelled plans, sorted by filename.
233: func (ps *PlanState) Cancelled() []PlanInfo {
234: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
235: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
236: 		if entry.Status != StatusCancelled {
237: 			continue
238: 		}
239: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
240: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
241: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
242: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
243: 		})
244: 	}
245: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
246: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
247: 	})
248: 	return result
249: }
250: 
251: // List returns all plans (including done and cancelled), sorted by filename.
252: func (ps *PlanState) List() []PlanInfo {
253: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0, len(ps.Plans))
254: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
255: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
256: 			Filename:    filename,
257: 			Status:      entry.Status,
258: 			Description: entry.Description,
259: 			Branch:      entry.Branch,
260: 			Topic:       entry.Topic,
261: 			CreatedAt:   entry.CreatedAt,
262: 		})
263: 	}
264: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
265: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
266: 	})
267: 	return result
268: }
269: 
270: // IsDone returns true only if the given plan has status StatusDone.
271: func (ps *PlanState) IsDone(filename string) bool {
272: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
273: 	if !ok {
274: 		return false
275: 	}
276: 	return entry.Status == StatusDone
277: }
278: 
279: // ForceSetStatus overrides a plan's status regardless of FSM rules.
280: // Validates the status is a known value. Use only for manual overrides (e.g. kq plan set-status --force).
281: func (ps *PlanState) ForceSetStatus(filename string, status Status) error {
282: 	if !isValidStatus(status) {
283: 		return fmt.Errorf("invalid status %q: must be one of ready, planning, implementing, reviewing, done, cancelled", status)
284: 	}
285: 	if _, ok := ps.Plans[filename]; !ok {
286: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
287: 	}
288: 	entry := ps.Plans[filename]
289: 	entry.Status = status
290: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
291: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
292: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
293: 	}
294: 	return nil
295: }
296: 
297: // isValidStatus returns true if s is a recognised lifecycle status.
298: func isValidStatus(s Status) bool {
299: 	switch s {
300: 	case StatusReady, StatusPlanning, StatusImplementing, StatusReviewing, StatusDone, StatusCancelled:
301: 		return true
302: 	}
303: 	return false
304: }
305: 
306: // setStatus updates a plan's status and persists to the store.
307: // Unexported: only for use within this package (tests). Production code must use planfsm.Transition.
308: func (ps *PlanState) setStatus(filename string, status Status) error {
309: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
310: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]PlanEntry)
311: 	}
312: 	entry := ps.Plans[filename]
313: 	entry.Status = status
314: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
315: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
316: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
317: 	}
318: 	return nil
319: }
320: 
321: // CreateWithContent adds a new plan entry with markdown content stored in the backend.
322: // The plan entry is created with StatusReady, and the content is persisted via store.SetContent.
323: // Returns an error if the plan already exists.
324: func (ps *PlanState) CreateWithContent(filename, description, branch, topic string, createdAt time.Time, content string) error {
325: 	if err := ps.Create(filename, description, branch, topic, createdAt); err != nil {
326: 		return err
327: 	}
328: 	if err := ps.store.SetContent(ps.project, filename, content); err != nil {
329: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store set content: %w", err)
330: 	}
331: 	return nil
332: }
333: 
334: // GetContent retrieves the markdown content for the given plan filename from the store.
335: func (ps *PlanState) GetContent(filename string) (string, error) {
336: 	return ps.store.GetContent(ps.project, filename)
337: }
338: 
339: // SetContent updates the markdown content for the given plan filename in the store.
340: func (ps *PlanState) SetContent(filename, content string) error {
341: 	return ps.store.SetContent(ps.project, filename, content)
342: }
343: 
344: // Create adds a new plan entry to the state and auto-creates the topic if needed.
345: func (ps *PlanState) Create(filename, description, branch, topic string, createdAt time.Time) error {
346: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
347: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]PlanEntry)
348: 	}
349: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[filename]; exists {
350: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan already exists: %s", filename)
351: 	}
352: 	entry := PlanEntry{
353: 		Status:      StatusReady,
354: 		Description: description,
355: 		Branch:      branch,
356: 		Topic:       topic,
357: 		CreatedAt:   createdAt.UTC(),
358: 	}
359: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
360: 	// Auto-create topic entry if it doesn't exist
361: 	if topic != "" {
362: 		if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
363: 			ps.TopicEntries = make(map[string]TopicEntry)
364: 		}
365: 		if _, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]; !exists {
366: 			ps.TopicEntries[topic] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: createdAt.UTC()}
367: 		}
368: 	}
369: 	if err := ps.store.Create(ps.project, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
370: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
371: 	}
372: 	// Auto-create topic in store if needed
373: 	if topic != "" {
374: 		topicEntry := planstore.TopicEntry{Name: topic, CreatedAt: createdAt.UTC()}
375: 		if err := ps.store.CreateTopic(ps.project, topicEntry); err != nil {
376: 			// Ignore "already exists" errors for topics
377: 			if !isAlreadyExistsError(err) {
378: 				return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
379: 			}
380: 		}
381: 	}
382: 	return nil
383: }
384: 
385: // Register adds a new plan entry with metadata and persists to the store.
386: // Returns an error if the plan already exists.
387: func (ps *PlanState) Register(filename, description, branch string, createdAt time.Time) error {
388: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
389: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]PlanEntry)
390: 	}
391: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[filename]; exists {
392: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan already exists: %s", filename)
393: 	}
394: 	entry := PlanEntry{
395: 		Status:      StatusReady,
396: 		Description: description,
397: 		Branch:      branch,
398: 		CreatedAt:   createdAt.UTC(),
399: 	}
400: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
401: 	if err := ps.store.Create(ps.project, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
402: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
403: 	}
404: 	return nil
405: }
406: 
407: // Entry returns the PlanEntry for the given filename, and whether it exists.
408: func (ps *PlanState) Entry(filename string) (PlanEntry, bool) {
409: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
410: 	return entry, ok
411: }
412: 
413: // SetTopic assigns a topic to an existing plan entry and persists to the store.
414: // If topic is non-empty and does not yet exist in TopicEntries, it is auto-created.
415: // Pass an empty string to remove the plan from any topic.
416: func (ps *PlanState) SetTopic(filename, topic string) error {
417: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
418: 	if !ok {
419: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
420: 	}
421: 	entry.Topic = topic
422: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
423: 	// Auto-create topic entry if it doesn't exist
424: 	if topic != "" {
425: 		if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
426: 			ps.TopicEntries = make(map[string]TopicEntry)
427: 		}
428: 		if _, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]; !exists {
429: 			ps.TopicEntries[topic] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC()}
430: 		}
431: 	}
432: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
433: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
434: 	}
435: 	// Auto-create topic in store if needed
436: 	if topic != "" {
437: 		topicEntry := planstore.TopicEntry{Name: topic, CreatedAt: ps.TopicEntries[topic].CreatedAt}
438: 		if err := ps.store.CreateTopic(ps.project, topicEntry); err != nil {
439: 			if !isAlreadyExistsError(err) {
440: 				return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
441: 			}
442: 		}
443: 	}
444: 	return nil
445: }
446: 
447: // SetBranch assigns a branch name to an existing plan entry and persists to the store.
448: func (ps *PlanState) SetBranch(filename, branch string) error {
449: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
450: 	if !ok {
451: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
452: 	}
453: 	entry.Branch = branch
454: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
455: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
456: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
457: 	}
458: 	return nil
459: }
460: 
461: // Save is a no-op — all mutations write through to the store immediately.
462: // Retained for API compatibility.
463: func (ps *PlanState) Save() error {
464: 	return nil
465: }
466: 
467: // DisplayName strips the date prefix and .md extension from a plan filename.
468: // "2026-02-20-my-feature.md" → "my-feature"
469: // "plain-plan.md" → "plain-plan"
470: func DisplayName(filename string) string {
471: 	name := strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
472: 	if len(name) > 11 && name[4] == '-' && name[7] == '-' && name[10] == '-' {
473: 		name = name[11:]
474: 	}
475: 	return name
476: }
477: 
478: // Rename renames a plan by giving it a new display name slug.
479: // It renames the .md file on disk (if it exists), rekeys the planstate entry,
480: // and persists the updated state. The date prefix of the old filename is preserved.
481: // newName should be a human-readable name (e.g., "auth refactor") which will be
482: // slugified automatically. Returns the new filename on success.
483: func (ps *PlanState) Rename(oldFilename, newName string) (string, error) {
484: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[oldFilename]
485: 	if !ok {
486: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", oldFilename)
487: 	}
488: 	if newName == "" {
489: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("new name cannot be empty")
490: 	}
491: 
492: 	// Build new filename preserving the date prefix (YYYY-MM-DD-) if present.
493: 	newSlug := slugify(newName)
494: 	if newSlug == "" {
495: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("new name produced an empty slug")
496: 	}
497: 	var newFilename string
498: 	if len(oldFilename) > 11 && oldFilename[4] == '-' && oldFilename[7] == '-' && oldFilename[10] == '-' {
499: 		newFilename = oldFilename[:11] + newSlug + ".md"
500: 	} else {
501: 		newFilename = newSlug + ".md"
502: 	}
503: 
504: 	if newFilename == oldFilename {
505: 		return oldFilename, nil // nothing to do
506: 	}
507: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[newFilename]; exists {
508: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("a plan named %q already exists", newFilename)
509: 	}
510: 
511: 	// Rename file on disk if it exists.
512: 	oldPath := filepath.Join(ps.Dir, oldFilename)
513: 	newPath := filepath.Join(ps.Dir, newFilename)
514: 	if _, err := os.Stat(oldPath); err == nil {
515: 		if err := os.Rename(oldPath, newPath); err != nil {
516: 			return "", fmt.Errorf("rename plan file: %w", err)
517: 		}
518: 	}
519: 
520: 	// Rekey the planstate entry.
521: 	ps.Plans[newFilename] = entry
522: 	delete(ps.Plans, oldFilename)
523: 
524: 	if err := ps.store.Rename(ps.project, oldFilename, newFilename); err != nil {
525: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
526: 	}
527: 	return newFilename, nil
528: }
529: 
530: // slugify converts a human name to a lowercase, hyphen-separated slug.
531: // "My Cool Feature!" → "my-cool-feature"
532: func slugify(name string) string {
533: 	name = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
534: 	// Replace any sequence of non-alphanumeric characters with a hyphen.
535: 	result := make([]rune, 0, len(name))
536: 	inHyphen := false
537: 	for _, r := range name {
538: 		if r >= 'a' && r <= 'z' || r >= '0' && r <= '9' {
539: 			result = append(result, r)
540: 			inHyphen = false
541: 		} else if !inHyphen && len(result) > 0 {
542: 			result = append(result, '-')
543: 			inHyphen = true
544: 		}
545: 	}
546: 	// Trim trailing hyphen.
547: 	for len(result) > 0 && result[len(result)-1] == '-' {
548: 		result = result[:len(result)-1]
549: 	}
550: 	return string(result)
551: }
552: 
553: // isAlreadyExistsError returns true if the error indicates a duplicate resource.
554: func isAlreadyExistsError(err error) bool {
555: 	if err == nil {
556: 		return false
557: 	}
558: 	msg := err.Error()
559: 	return strings.Contains(msg, "already exists")
560: }
561: 
562: // toPlanstoreEntry converts a local PlanEntry to a planstore.PlanEntry for
563: // writing to the store.
564: func (ps *PlanState) toPlanstoreEntry(filename string, e PlanEntry) planstore.PlanEntry {
565: 	return planstore.PlanEntry{
566: 		Filename:    filename,
567: 		Status:      planstore.Status(e.Status),
568: 		Description: e.Description,
569: 		Branch:      e.Branch,
570: 		Topic:       e.Topic,
571: 		CreatedAt:   e.CreatedAt,
572: 		Implemented: e.Implemented,
573: 	}
574: }

(End of file - total 574 lines)

	description TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
	branch      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
	topic       TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',

10: // buildTaskPrompt constructs the prompt for a single task instance.
11: func buildTaskPrompt(plan *planparser.Plan, task planparser.Task, waveNumber, totalWaves, peerCount int) string {
12: 	var sb strings.Builder
13: 
14: 	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Implement Task %d: %s\n\n", task.Number, task.Title))
15: 	sb.WriteString("Load the `kasmos-coder` skill before starting. Also load `cli-tools` for the tool selection reference.\n\n")
16: 
17: 	// Plan context
18: 	header := plan.HeaderContext()
19: 	if header != "" {
20: 		sb.WriteString("## Plan Context\n\n")
21: 		sb.WriteString(header)
22: 		sb.WriteString("\n")
23: 	}
24: 
25: 	// Wave context
26: 	sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("## Wave %d of %d\n\n", waveNumber, totalWaves))
27: 
28: 	// Parallel awareness — only for multi-task waves
29: 	if peerCount > 1 {
30: 		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("## Parallel Execution\n\n"))
31: 		sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("You are Task %d of %d in Wave %d. %d other agents are working in parallel on this same worktree.\n\n",
32: 			task.Number, peerCount, waveNumber, peerCount-1))
33: 
34: 		sb.WriteString("Your assigned files are listed in the Task Instructions below. Prioritize those files. ")
35: 		sb.WriteString("If you must touch a shared file (go.mod, go.sum, imports), make minimal surgical changes - ")
36: 		sb.WriteString("do not reorganize, reformat, or refactor anything outside your task scope.\n\n")
37: 
38: 		sb.WriteString("CRITICAL - shared worktree rules:\n")
39: 		sb.WriteString("- NEVER run `git add .` or `git add -A` - you will commit other agents' in-progress work\n")
40: 		sb.WriteString("- NEVER run `git stash` or `git reset` - you will destroy sibling agents' changes\n")
41: 		sb.WriteString("- NEVER run `git checkout -- <file>` on files you didn't modify - you will revert a sibling's edits\n")
42: 		sb.WriteString("- NEVER run formatters/linters across the whole project (e.g. `go fmt ./...`) - scope them to your files only\n")
43: 		sb.WriteString("- NEVER try to fix test failures in files outside your task - they may be caused by incomplete parallel work\n")
44: 		sb.WriteString("- DO `git add` only the specific files you changed\n")
45: 		sb.WriteString("- DO commit frequently with your task number in the message\n")
46: 		sb.WriteString("- DO expect untracked files and uncommitted changes that are not yours - ignore them\n\n")
47: 	}
48: 
49: 	// Task body
50: 	sb.WriteString("## Task Instructions\n\n")
51: 	sb.WriteString(task.Body)
52: 	sb.WriteString("\n")
53: 
54: 	return sb.String()
55: }

parts.text:
Before writing the plan, I have a few clarifying questions about the scope and design.

**Question 1:** The plan content embeds the ClickUp task ID as `**Source:** ClickUp <ID> (<URL>)` in the markdown. Currently there's no structured field for the ClickUp task ID in the plan store schema or `PlanEntry`. Should we:

**A (recommended):** Parse the ClickUp task ID from the plan content on-demand (when we need to post a comment). This avoids a schema migration and works for all existing imported plans.

**B:** Add a `clickup_task_id` column to the plan store schema and populate it during import. Cleaner long-term but requires migration + backfill.

**C:** Both — parse from content as fallback, but also store it structurally for new imports.

My recommendation is **A** — it's simpler, works retroactively, and the parse is trivial (regex on plan content). We can always add structured storage later if needed.

diffs.before:
---
name: cli-tools
description: Use when performing code search, refactoring, file transformations, diffing, string replacement, structured data processing, spell checking, or codebase metrics — or when about to reach for grep, sed, awk, find, diff, or wc.
---

# CLI Tools

Modern CLI tools that replace legacy unix utilities for code-related tasks. These tools understand syntax, structure, and context — producing safer, faster, and more accurate results than regex-based alternatives.

<HARD-GATE>
## Banned Tools

These legacy tools are NEVER permitted. Using them is a violation, not a preference.

| Banned | Replacement | No Exceptions |
|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `grep` | `rg` (ripgrep) | Even for simple one-liners. `rg` is faster, respects .gitignore, and handles encoding correctly |
| `grep -r` | `rg` | Recursive grep is still grep. Always `rg` |
| `grep -E` | `rg` | Extended regex is still grep. `rg` supports the same patterns |
| `sed` | `sd` | Even for one-liners. `sd` has saner syntax and no delimiter escaping |
| `awk` | `yq`/`jq` (structured) or `sd` (text) | No awk for any purpose |
| `find` | `fd` or glob tools | Even for simple file listing. `fd` respects .gitignore; use `fd -e go` for extension |
| `diff` (standalone) | `difft` | `git diff` is fine — standalone `diff` is not |
| `wc -l` | `scc` | Even for single files |

**`git diff` is allowed** — it's a git subcommand, not standalone `diff`. Use `GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF=difft git diff` when reviewing code changes.

**STOP.** If you are about to type `grep`, `sed`, `awk`, `find`, `diff`, or `wc` — stop and use the replacement. There are no exceptions. "Just this once" is a violation.
</HARD-GATE>

<HARD-GATE>
## Batch Edit Rule

When you need to make the **same or similar change across multiple files**, you MUST use a CLI tool (`sd`, `comby`, or `ast-grep`) instead of repeated Edit tool calls. This is not a suggestion.

| Change type | Tool | Example |
|-------------|------|---------|
| Literal string replacement across files | `sd` | `sd 'oldFunc' 'newFunc' src/**/*.go` |
| Same structural pattern across files | `comby` | `comby 'before(:[args])' 'after(:[args])' .go -in-place` |
| Symbol rename across files | `ast-grep` | `ast-grep run --pattern 'old($$$)' --rewrite 'new($$$)' --lang go` |
| Same line deletion across files | `sd` | `sd 'lineToRemove\n' '' src/**/*.go` |

**The threshold is 3.** If you are about to make the same Edit in 3+ files, stop and use a CLI tool instead. One command replaces N edit calls — it's faster, less error-prone, and produces smaller diffs.

**Red flags** — if you catch yourself doing any of these, you are violating this rule:
- Copy-pasting the same `oldString`/`newString` into multiple Edit calls
- Making a list of files to edit one-by-one with the same pattern
- "I'll just do this manually, it's only N files" (if N ≥ 3, use a tool)
- Using Edit with `replaceAll: true` across multiple files for the same string

**STOP.** If you are about to make the same edit in 3+ files — stop and use `sd`, `comby`, or `ast-grep`. There are no exceptions.
</HARD-GATE>

## Tool Selection by Task

Pick the right tool for the job. When multiple tools could work, the table shows the preferred choice and why.

| Task | Use | Not | Why |
|------|-----|-----|-----|
| Rename symbol across files | `ast-grep` | `sed`/`sd` | AST-aware, won't rename inside strings/comments |
| Structural code rewrite | `comby` | `sed`/`awk` | Understands balanced delimiters, nesting |
| Find code pattern | `ast-grep --pattern` | `grep`/`rg` | Matches syntax, not text |
| Find literal string | `rg` | `grep` | Fast, respects .gitignore, correct encoding |
| Find files by name/extension | `fd` | `find` | Respects .gitignore, simpler syntax |
| Replace string in files | `sd` | `sed` | No delimiter escaping, sane defaults |
| Read/modify YAML/TOML/JSON | `yq` / `jq` | `sed`/`awk` | Understands structure, preserves formatting |
| Review code changes | `difft` | `diff` | Syntax-aware, ignores formatting noise |
| Spell check code | `typos` | manual | Understands camelCase, identifiers |
| Count lines / codebase metrics | `scc` | `wc -l`/`cloc` | Fast, includes complexity estimates |
| Multi-line structural rewrite | `comby` | `sed` | Handles cross-line patterns with holes |
| Add/change function params | `comby` | manual | `func F(:[params])` patterns |
| Simple literal find-replace | `sd` | `sed` | Simpler syntax, in-place by default |

## ast-grep vs comby Decision

Both do structural code operations. Choose based on the transformation:

| Scenario | Winner | Reason |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Rename all calls to a function | ast-grep | AST-level symbol matching |
| Find all usages of a pattern | ast-grep | Tree-sitter precision |
| Add/change function parameters | comby | Hole syntax `:[params]` is natural |
| Replace entire function body | comby | `{:[_]}` discards balanced block |
| Insert code after a function | comby | Match + append in rewrite |
| Add to import block | comby | `import (:[imports])` pattern |
| Multi-line structural rewrite | comby | Balanced delimiter matching |
| Lint-style pattern detection | ast-grep | Rule-based scanning |

## Quick Reference

### rg (ripgrep)

Fast text search that respects .gitignore. Primary replacement for all `grep` variants.

```bash
rg 'pattern' [path]                            # basic search (recursive by default)
rg 'pattern' --type go                         # filter by language type
rg 'pattern' -g '*.go'                         # filter by glob
rg 'pattern' -g '*.go' -g '!*_test.go'         # include/exclude globs
rg -i 'pattern'                                # case insensitive
rg -F 'literal[0]'                             # fixed string (no regex)
rg -U 'start\n.*end'                           # multiline match
rg -l 'pattern'                                # list matching files only
rg -c 'pattern'                                # count matches per file
rg --no-heading -n 'pattern'                   # machine-readable output (file:line:match)
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/rg.md](resources/rg.md)

### ast-grep (`ast-grep`)

AST-based code search and replace using tree-sitter. Matches syntax structure, not text.

```bash
ast-grep run --pattern 'fmt.Errorf($$$)' --lang go          # find all calls
ast-grep run --pattern 'errors.New($A)' --rewrite 'fmt.Errorf($A)' --lang go  # replace
ast-grep run --pattern '$A != nil' --lang go src/            # search specific path
```

Key patterns: `$VAR` (single node), `$$$` (variable-length args), `$_` (wildcard)

**In-depth reference:** [resources/ast-grep.md](resources/ast-grep.md)

### comby (`comby`)

Structural search/replace that understands balanced delimiters, strings, and comments. Superior to regex for multi-line code transformations.

```bash
comby 'func :[name](:[args]) {:[body]}' 'func :[name](:[args]) error {:[body]}' .go -in-place
comby 'import (:[imports])' 'import (:[imports]
	"new/package"
)' file.go -in-place
```

**Critical rule:** Always use `{:[body]}` (inline braces), never split `{` and `}` on separate lines.

Key holes: `:[var]` (everything), `:[[var]]` (word), `:[_]` (unnamed wildcard), `:[var\n]` (to newline)

**In-depth reference:** [resources/comby.md](resources/comby.md) — read this before any non-trivial comby usage. Contains critical pitfalls around whitespace normalization and balanced delimiter matching.

### difftastic (`difft`)

Syntax-aware structural diff. Dramatically cleaner output for refactors and moves.

```bash
difft old.go new.go                              # compare files
GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF=difft git diff                  # git integration
GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF=difft git log -p -- path/file   # file history
difft --display inline old.go new.go              # single-column output
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/difftastic.md](resources/difftastic.md)

### sd (`sd`)

Modern sed alternative. In-place by default, no delimiter escaping needed.

```bash
sd 'old_name' 'new_name' src/**/*.go              # replace in files
sd 'version = "(\d+)"' 'version = "2"' file.toml  # regex with groups
sd -p 'foo' 'bar' file.txt                         # preview (dry run)
sd -F 'literal[0]' 'replacement' file.txt          # fixed string mode
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/sd.md](resources/sd.md)

### yq (`yq`)

YAML/JSON processor (jq wrapper for YAML). Query and modify structured config files.

```bash
yq '.metadata.name' file.yaml                     # read field
yq -y '.key = "value"' file.yaml                   # modify YAML
yq -y '.dependencies += {"new": "^1.0"}' pkg.yaml  # add to map
cat file.yaml | yq '.items[].name'                 # query array
```

Note: This is the Python-based `yq` (kislyuk/yq), a jq wrapper. Uses jq filter syntax. Use `-y` flag for YAML output, otherwise outputs JSON.

**In-depth reference:** [resources/yq.md](resources/yq.md)

### typos (`typos`)

Fast source code spell checker. Understands identifiers, camelCase, filenames.

```bash
typos                          # check entire project
typos src/                     # check specific path
typos --write-changes          # auto-fix typos
typos --format brief           # compact output
```

Run before commits to catch typos in identifiers, strings, and comments.

**In-depth reference:** [resources/typos.md](resources/typos.md)

### scc (`scc`)

Fast code counter with complexity estimates. Useful for project scope assessment.

```bash
scc                                  # full project summary
scc internal/                        # specific directory
scc --by-file --sort lines           # per-file breakdown
scc --not-match '_test.go$'          # exclude test files
scc --include-ext go,ts              # specific languages only
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/scc.md](resources/scc.md)

## Violations

These violations are not suggestions. If you do any of these, you are violating the skill.

| Violation | Required Fix |
|-----------|-------------|
| Using `grep` for anything | Use `rg` for text search, `ast-grep` for code patterns |
| Using `grep -r` (recursive) | Use `rg` — recursive by default, also respects .gitignore |
| Using `grep -E` (extended regex) | Use `rg` — supports full regex including extended patterns |
| Using `sed` for anything | Use `sd` for replacements, `ast-grep`/`comby` for refactoring |
| Using `awk` for anything | Use `yq`/`jq` for structured data, `sd` for text processing |
| Using `find` for anything | Use `fd` for file finding, glob tools for path patterns |
| Using standalone `diff` | Use `difft` for syntax-aware structural diffs |
| Using `wc -l` for counting | Use `scc` for language-aware counts + complexity |
| Splitting `{` / `}` in comby templates | Always inline: `{:[body]}` not `{\n:[body]\n}` |
| Forgetting `-in-place` with comby | Without it, comby only previews changes |
| Repeated Edit calls with same pattern across 3+ files | Use `sd`, `comby`, or `ast-grep` — one command, not N edits |
| Using Edit `replaceAll` across multiple files for same string | Use `sd 'old' 'new' file1 file2 ...` or glob pattern |

| `find` | `fd` or glob tools | Even for simple file listing. `fd` respects .gitignore; use `fd -e go` for extension |
| `diff` (standalone) | `difft` | `git diff` is fine — standalone `diff` is not |
| `wc -l` | `scc` | Even for single files |

**`git diff` is allowed** — it's a git subcommand, not standalone `diff`. Use `GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF=difft git diff` when reviewing code changes.

**STOP.** If you are about to type `grep`, `sed`, `awk`, `find`, `diff`, or `wc` — stop and use the replacement. There are no exceptions. "Just this once" is a violation.
</HARD-GATE>

## Tool Selection by Task

Pick the right tool for the job. When multiple tools could work, the table shows the preferred choice and why.

| Task | Use | Not | Why |
|------|-----|-----|-----|
| Rename symbol across files | `ast-grep` | `sed`/`sd` | AST-aware, won't rename inside strings/comments |
| Structural code rewrite | `comby` | `sed`/`awk` | Understands balanced delimiters, nesting |
| Find code pattern | `ast-grep --pattern` | `grep`/`rg` | Matches syntax, not text |
| Find literal string | `rg` | `grep` | Fast, respects .gitignore, correct encoding |
| Find files by name/extension | `fd` | `find` | Respects .gitignore, simpler syntax |
| Replace string in files | `sd` | `sed` | No delimiter escaping, sane defaults |
| Read/modify YAML/TOML/JSON | `yq` / `jq` | `sed`/`awk` | Understands structure, preserves formatting |
| Review code changes | `difft` | `diff` | Syntax-aware, ignores formatting noise |
| Spell check code | `typos` | manual | Understands camelCase, identifiers |
| Count lines / codebase metrics | `scc` | `wc -l`/`cloc` | Fast, includes complexity estimates |
| Multi-line structural rewrite | `comby` | `sed` | Handles cross-line patterns with holes |
| Add/change function params | `comby` | manual | `func F(:[params])` patterns |
| Simple literal find-replace | `sd` | `sed` | Simpler syntax, in-place by default |

## ast-grep vs comby Decision

Both do structural code operations. Choose based on the transformation:

| Scenario | Winner | Reason |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Rename all calls to a function | ast-grep | AST-level symbol matching |
| Find all usages of a pattern | ast-grep | Tree-sitter precision |
| Add/change function parameters | comby | Hole syntax `:[params]` is natural |
| Replace entire function body | comby | `{:[_]}` discards balanced block |
| Insert code after a function | comby | Match + append in rewrite |
| Add to import block | comby | `import (:[imports])` pattern |
| Multi-line structural rewrite | comby | Balanced delimiter matching |
| Lint-style pattern detection | ast-grep | Rule-based scanning |

## Quick Reference

### rg (ripgrep)

Fast text search that respects .gitignore. Primary replacement for all `grep` variants.

```bash
rg 'pattern' [path]                            # basic search (recursive by default)
rg 'pattern' --type go                         # filter by language type
rg 'pattern' -g '*.go'                         # filter by glob
rg 'pattern' -g '*.go' -g '!*_test.go'         # include/exclude globs
rg -i 'pattern'                                # case insensitive
rg -F 'literal[0]'                             # fixed string (no regex)
rg -U 'start\n.*end'                           # multiline match
rg -l 'pattern'                                # list matching files only
rg -c 'pattern'                                # count matches per file
rg --no-heading -n 'pattern'                   # machine-readable output (file:line:match)
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/rg.md](resources/rg.md)

### ast-grep (`ast-grep`)

AST-based code search and replace using tree-sitter. Matches syntax structure, not text.

```bash
ast-grep run --pattern 'fmt.Errorf($$$)' --lang go          # find all calls
ast-grep run --pattern 'errors.New($A)' --rewrite 'fmt.Errorf($A)' --lang go  # replace
ast-grep run --pattern '$A != nil' --lang go src/            # search specific path
```

Key patterns: `$VAR` (single node), `$$$` (variable-length args), `$_` (wildcard)

**In-depth reference:** [resources/ast-grep.md](resources/ast-grep.md)

### comby (`comby`)

Structural search/replace that understands balanced delimiters, strings, and comments. Superior to regex for multi-line code transformations.

```bash
comby 'func :[name](:[args]) {:[body]}' 'func :[name](:[args]) error {:[body]}' .go -in-place
comby 'import (:[imports])' 'import (:[imports]
	"new/package"
)' file.go -in-place
```

**Critical rule:** Always use `{:[body]}` (inline braces), never split `{` and `}` on separate lines.

Key holes: `:[var]` (everything), `:[[var]]` (word), `:[_]` (unnamed wildcard), `:[var\n]` (to newline)

**In-depth reference:** [resources/comby.md](resources/comby.md) — read this before any non-trivial comby usage. Contains critical pitfalls around whitespace normalization and balanced delimiter matching.

### difftastic (`difft`)

Syntax-aware structural diff. Dramatically cleaner output for refactors and moves.

```bash
difft old.go new.go                              # compare files
GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF=difft git diff                  # git integration
GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF=difft git log -p -- path/file   # file history
difft --display inline old.go new.go              # single-column output
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/difftastic.md](resources/difftastic.md)

### sd (`sd`)

Modern sed alternative. In-place by default, no delimiter escaping needed.

```bash
sd 'old_name' 'new_name' src/**/*.go              # replace in files
sd 'version = "(\d+)"' 'version = "2"' file.toml  # regex with groups
sd -p 'foo' 'bar' file.txt                         # preview (dry run)
sd -F 'literal[0]' 'replacement' file.txt          # fixed string mode
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/sd.md](resources/sd.md)

### yq (`yq`)

YAML/JSON processor (jq wrapper for YAML). Query and modify structured config files.

```bash
yq '.metadata.name' file.yaml                     # read field
yq -y '.key = "value"' file.yaml                   # modify YAML
yq -y '.dependencies += {"new": "^1.0"}' pkg.yaml  # add to map
cat file.yaml | yq '.items[].name'                 # query array
```

Note: This is the Python-based `yq` (kislyuk/yq), a jq wrapper. Uses jq filter syntax. Use `-y` flag for YAML output, otherwise outputs JSON.

**In-depth reference:** [resources/yq.md](resources/yq.md)

### typos (`typos`)

Fast source code spell checker. Understands identifiers, camelCase, filenames.

```bash
typos                          # check entire project
typos src/                     # check specific path
typos --write-changes          # auto-fix typos
typos --format brief           # compact output
```

Run before commits to catch typos in identifiers, strings, and comments.

**In-depth reference:** [resources/typos.md](resources/typos.md)

### scc (`scc`)

Fast code counter with complexity estimates. Useful for project scope assessment.

```bash
scc                                  # full project summary
scc internal/                        # specific directory
scc --by-file --sort lines           # per-file breakdown
scc --not-match '_test.go$'          # exclude test files
scc --include-ext go,ts              # specific languages only
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/scc.md](resources/scc.md)

## Violations

These violations are not suggestions. If you do any of these, you are violating the skill.

| Violation | Required Fix |
|-----------|-------------|
| Using `grep` for anything | Use `rg` for text search, `ast-grep` for code patterns |
| Using `grep -r` (recursive) | Use `rg` — recursive by default, also respects .gitignore |
| Using `grep -E` (extended regex) | Use `rg` — supports full regex including extended patterns |
| Using `sed` for anything | Use `sd` for replacements, `ast-grep`/`comby` for refactoring |
| Using `awk` for anything | Use `yq`/`jq` for structured data, `sd` for text processing |
| Using `find` for anything | Use `fd` for file finding, glob tools for path patterns |
| Using standalone `diff` | Use `difft` for syntax-aware structural diffs |
| Using `wc -l` for counting | Use `scc` for language-aware counts + complexity |
| Splitting `{` / `}` in comby templates | Always inline: `{:[body]}` not `{\n:[body]\n}` |
| Forgetting `-in-place` with comby | Without it, comby only previews changes |

# kasmos Agents

## Coder
Implementation agent. Writes code, fixes bugs, runs tests.
Follow TDD: write failing test first, implement, verify green.
Load superpowers skills: `test-driven-development`, `systematic-debugging`, `verification-before-completion`.
Load project skills: `tui-design` (TUI components), `tmux-orchestration` (tmux/worker code), `golang-pro` (Go patterns).

## Reviewer
Review agent. Checks quality, security, spec compliance.
Use `difft` for structural diffs (not line-based `git diff`).
Use `sg` (ast-grep) to verify patterns across the codebase.
Load superpowers skills: `requesting-code-review`, `receiving-code-review`.
Load project skills: `tui-design` (always for TUI/UX reviews), `tmux-orchestration` (when reviewing tmux/worker code).

## Planner
Planning agent. Writes specs, plans, decomposes work into packages.
Use `scc` for codebase metrics when scoping work.
Load superpowers skills: `brainstorming`, `writing-plans`.
Load project skills: `tui-design` (always for TUI work), `tmux-orchestration` (when task involves tmux/worker lifecycle).

## Plan State (CRITICAL)
Plans live in `docs/plans/`. State tracked in `docs/plans/plan-state.json`.
Never modify plan files for state tracking. **You MUST register every plan** by adding
an entry to `plan-state.json` with `"status": "ready"` immediately after writing the `.md` file.
Unregistered plans are invisible in the kasmos sidebar.
Valid statuses: `ready` → `in_progress` → `done`. Only kasmos transitions beyond `done`.

## CLI Tools

Read the `cli-tools` skill (SKILL.md) at session start. Read individual
resource files in `resources/` when using that specific tool.

diffs.before:
---
description: General-purpose assistant - answers questions, explores code, quick tasks
mode: primary
---

You are the chat agent. Help the user understand their codebase, answer questions, and handle quick one-off tasks.

## Role

You are a general-purpose assistant for interactive use. Unlike the coder agent (which follows TDD workflows and formal processes), you optimize for fast, accurate responses in conversation.

- Answer questions about the codebase — architecture, patterns, dependencies, how things work
- Do quick one-off tasks — rename a variable, add a comment, check a type signature
- Explore and explain — trace call chains, find usages, summarize modules
- For substantial implementation work, delegate to the coder agent

## Guidelines

- Be concise. The user is asking interactively, not requesting a report.
- Read code before answering questions about it. Don't guess from filenames.
- When a task grows beyond a quick fix, say so and suggest using the coder agent instead.
- Use project skills when they're relevant, but don't load heavy workflows (TDD, debugging) for simple questions.

## CLI Tools (MANDATORY)

You MUST read the `cli-tools` skill (SKILL.md) at the start of every session.
When making the same change across 3+ files, use `sd`/`comby`/`ast-grep` — not repeated Edit calls.
It contains tool selection tables, quick references, and common mistakes for
ast-grep, comby, difftastic, sd, yq, typos, and scc. The deep-dive reference
files in `resources/` should be read when you need to use that specific tool —
you don't need to read all of them upfront.

---
description: Implementation agent - writes code, fixes bugs, runs tests
mode: primary
---

You are the coder agent. Implement features, fix bugs, and write tests.

## Workflow

Before writing code, load the `kasmos-coder` skill.

## Plan State

Plan state is stored in the **plan store** (SQLite database or HTTP API), not in files on disk.
Use `kas plan` CLI commands for all state mutations. When you finish implementing a plan,
transition it via `kas plan set-status <plan> done --force`. Valid statuses: `ready`, `planning`,
`implementing`, `reviewing`, `done`, `cancelled`.

## CLI Tools (MANDATORY)

You MUST read the `cli-tools` skill (SKILL.md) at the start of every session.
It contains tool selection tables, quick references, and common mistakes for
ast-grep, comby, difftastic, sd, yq, typos, and scc. The deep-dive reference
files in `resources/` should be read when you need to use that specific tool —
you don't need to read all of them upfront.

**Batch edit rule:** When making the same change across 3+ files, you MUST use
`sd`, `comby`, or `ast-grep` instead of repeated Edit tool calls. One CLI command
replaces N edits. This is enforced — see the Batch Edit Rule in the cli-tools skill.

## Parallel Execution

You may be running alongside other agents on a shared worktree. When `KASMOS_TASK` is set,
you are one of several concurrent agents — each assigned a specific task. Expect dirty git state
from sibling agents (untracked files, uncommitted changes in files you don't own).
Focus exclusively on your assigned task. The dynamic prompt you receive has specific rules.

diffs.before:
---
description: Fixer agent - debugging, investigation, state resets, cleanup, branch management
mode: primary
---

You are the fixer agent. Debug issues, investigate failures, and handle operational fixes in the kasmos workflow.

Load the `kasmos-fixer` skill.

## Scaffolding System (first step for skills/agent commands)

Before modifying any skill or agent command, check whether it is scaffold-managed and update the scaffold source first:
- Skills: `.agents/skills/...` is canonical, and `internal/initcmd/scaffold/templates/skills/...` must stay in sync.
- Agent prompts: update `internal/initcmd/scaffold/templates/{opencode,claude}/agents/...` and mirror local prompt files (for example `.opencode/agents/...`) in this repo.
- Agent commands: if a command has a scaffold template under `internal/initcmd/scaffold/templates/...`, update the template and the live command file together.

Do not patch only one copy when a scaffold source exists.

## Role

You are a debugger, investigator, and operational troubleshooter. You investigate test failures,
trace root causes, fix stuck plan states, clean up stale worktrees and branches, and triage
loose ends. You do NOT plan features, write implementation code, or review PRs.

## Operations

Use `kas plan` CLI for all plan state mutations:
- `kas plan list [--status <status>]` — show plans and filter by status
- `kas plan set-status <plan> <status> --force` — force-override a plan's status
- `kas plan transition <plan> <event>` — apply a valid FSM event
- `kas plan implement <plan> [--wave N]` — trigger wave implementation

Use raw git/gh for branch and worktree operations:
- `git worktree list` / `git worktree remove <path>` — manage worktrees
- `git branch -d <branch>` — clean up branches
- `gh pr create` — create pull requests
- `git merge` — merge branches

## Slash Commands

These commands are available for one-shot operations:
- `/kas.reset-plan <plan-file> <status>` — force-reset a plan's status
- `/kas.finish-branch [plan-file]` — merge to main or create PR
- `/kas.cleanup [--dry-run]` — remove stale worktrees and orphan branches
- `/kas.implement <plan-file> [--wave N]` — trigger wave implementation
- `/kas.triage` — bulk scan and triage plans

## Release Operations

When creating a release tag, you **must** bump the `version` constant in `main.go` first.
The CI/CD `Release` workflow validates that the tag matches `main.go` — mismatches fail the build.

```bash
# 1. determine new version
NEW_VERSION="X.Y.Z"

# 2. bump version in main.go (line 25: version = "...")
sd 'version\s*=\s*"[^"]*"' "version     = \"${NEW_VERSION}\"" main.go

# 3. verify it matches
rg '^\s*version\s*=' main.go

# 4. commit the bump on main
git add main.go
git commit -m "chore: bump version to ${NEW_VERSION}"

# 5. tag and push
git tag "v${NEW_VERSION}"
git push origin main "v${NEW_VERSION}"
```

**Never push a `v*` tag without first verifying `main.go` version matches.**

## Behavior

- Always confirm what you're about to do before doing it (one-line summary)
- Report what changed after each operation
- Investigate before proposing fixes — evidence first
- Be terse — no walls of text, just action and result

## CLI Tools (MANDATORY)

You MUST read the `cli-tools` skill (SKILL.md) at the start of every session.
When making the same change across 3+ files, use `sd`/`comby`/`ast-grep` — not repeated Edit calls.

---
description: Planning agent - specs, plans, task decomposition
mode: primary
---

You are the planner agent. Write specs, implementation plans, and decompose work into packages.

## Workflow

Before planning, load the `kasmos-planner` skill.

## Plan Review (MANDATORY)

After writing a plan, you MUST run the plan review checklist from the `kasmos-planner`
skill before committing or signaling. Do not skip this step. Fix all failures inline
before proceeding.

## Branch Policy

Always commit plan files to the main branch. Do NOT create feature branches for planning work.
The feature branch for implementation is created by kasmos when the user triggers "implement".

Only register implementation plans — never register design docs (*-design.md) as separate entries.

## Plan Registration (CRITICAL — must follow every time)

Plan state is stored in the **plan store** (SQLite database or HTTP API), not in files on disk.
Never modify plan state directly — use `kas plan` CLI commands or sentinel files.

**You MUST register every plan you write.** How you register depends on the environment.

Registration steps (do both, never skip step 2):
1. Write the plan content
2. Register the plan — check `$KASMOS_MANAGED` to determine method:

**If `KASMOS_MANAGED=1` (running inside kasmos):** Create a sentinel file:
`.kasmos/signals/planner-finished-<date>-<name>.md` (empty file — just `touch` it).
kasmos will detect this and register the plan. **Do not modify plan state directly.**

**If `KASMOS_MANAGED` is unset (raw terminal):** Use the CLI to register:
`kas plan register <date>-<name>.md`

**Never modify plan statuses directly.** Only register NEW plans. Status transitions (`ready` →
`implementing` → `reviewing` → `done`) are managed by kasmos or the relevant workflow skill.

## CLI Tools (MANDATORY)

You MUST read the `cli-tools` skill (SKILL.md) at the start of every session.
When making the same change across 3+ files, use `sd`/`comby`/`ast-grep` — not repeated Edit calls.
It contains tool selection tables, quick references, and common mistakes for
ast-grep, comby, difftastic, sd, yq, typos, and scc. The deep-dive reference
files in `resources/` should be read when you need to use that specific tool —
you don't need to read all of them upfront.

`kas plan register <date>-<name>.md`

**Never modify plan statuses directly.** Only register NEW plans. Status transitions (`ready` →
`implementing` → `reviewing` → `done`) are managed by kasmos or the relevant workflow skill.

## CLI Tools (MANDATORY)

You MUST read the `cli-tools` skill (SKILL.md) at the start of every session.
It contains tool selection tables, quick references, and common mistakes for
ast-grep, comby, difftastic, sd, yq, typos, and scc. The deep-dive reference
files in `resources/` should be read when you need to use that specific tool —
you don't need to read all of them upfront.

# Update ClickUp with Progress Implementation Plan

**Goal:** When a kasmos plan originates from a ClickUp task, automatically post progress comments back to that ClickUp task at key lifecycle milestones — plan finalized, wave completed (multi-wave only), review findings, and fixer results.

**Architecture:** Add a `clickup_task_id` column to the plan store schema (with migration). Store the ClickUp task ID during import and parse it from existing plan content via a backfill CLI command. Add a `Commenter` type to `internal/clickup` that posts markdown comments via the ClickUp MCP `clickup_create_task_comment` tool. Hook the commenter into the TUI's existing lifecycle event handlers (signal processing, wave completion, review signals) as fire-and-forget async `tea.Cmd`s. The commenter is nil-safe — plans without a ClickUp task ID are silently skipped.

**Tech Stack:** Go, SQLite (plan store migration), ClickUp MCP (`clickup_create_task_comment`), bubbletea async commands

**Size:** Medium (estimated ~4 hours, 5 tasks, 2 waves)

---

## Wave 1: Data Layer + Commenter

### Task 1: Add clickup_task_id to plan store schema and wire through all layers

**Files:**
- Modify: `config/planstore/store.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/sqlite.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/http.go`
- Modify: `config/planstore/server.go`
- Modify: `config/planstate/planstate.go`
- Test: `config/planstore/sqlite_test.go`
- Test: `config/planstate/planstate_test.go`

**Step 1: write the failing test**

```go
// In config/planstore/sqlite_test.go
func TestClickUpTaskIDPersistence(t *testing.T) {
    store := newTestStore(t)
    entry := PlanEntry{
        Filename:      "test-plan.md",
        Status:        StatusReady,
        ClickUpTaskID: "abc123",
    }
    require.NoError(t, store.Create("proj", entry))
    got, err := store.Get("proj", "test-plan.md")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    assert.Equal(t, "abc123", got.ClickUpTaskID)
}
```

**Step 2: run test to verify it fails**

```bash
go test ./config/planstore/... -run TestClickUpTaskIDPersistence -v
```

expected: FAIL — `ClickUpTaskID` field does not exist

**Step 3: write minimal implementation**

1. Add `ClickUpTaskID string` field to `PlanEntry` in `config/planstore/store.go`.
2. Add SQLite migration in `config/planstore/sqlite.go`: `ALTER TABLE plans ADD COLUMN clickup_task_id TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''`. Follow the existing `migrateAddContentColumn` pattern — check PRAGMA table_info first, add column only if missing.
3. Update all SQL queries in `sqlite.go` (Create, Get, Update, List, ListByStatus, ListByTopic, scanPlanEntry, scanPlanEntries) to include the new column.
4. Add `ClickUpTaskID` to `PlanEntry` in `config/planstate/planstate.go` and wire it through `toPlanstoreEntry` and `Load`. Also add a `SetClickUpTaskID(filename, taskID string) error` method that updates the field and persists via store (mirrors the existing `SetBranch`/`SetTopic` pattern).
5. Update `config/planstore/http.go` — the field is already JSON-serialized via the `PlanEntry` struct, so HTTP transport works automatically.
6. Update `config/planstore/server.go` — same, no explicit changes needed beyond what the struct provides.

**Step 4: run test to verify it passes**

```bash
go test ./config/planstore/... -run TestClickUpTaskIDPersistence -v
```

expected: PASS

**Step 5: commit**

```bash
git add config/planstore/store.go config/planstore/sqlite.go config/planstore/http.go config/planstore/server.go config/planstate/planstate.go config/planstore/sqlite_test.go config/planstate/planstate_test.go
git commit -m "feat(planstore): add clickup_task_id column with migration"
```

### Task 2: ClickUp Commenter and content-based task ID parser

**Files:**
- Create: `internal/clickup/commenter.go`
- Create: `internal/clickup/commenter_test.go`

**Step 1: write the failing test**

```go
func TestCommenterPostComment(t *testing.T) {
    mock := &mockMCPCaller{
        tools: []mcpclient.Tool{{Name: "clickup_create_task_comment"}},
    }
    c := NewCommenter(mock)
    err := c.PostComment("abc123", "test comment")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    assert.Equal(t, "clickup_create_task_comment", mock.lastToolName)
    assert.Equal(t, "abc123", mock.lastArgs["task_id"])
    assert.Equal(t, "test comment", mock.lastArgs["comment_text"])

func TestCommenterNilSafe(t *testing.T) {

    var c *Commenter
    err := c.PostComment("abc123", "test")
    assert.NoError(t, err)
}
```

- `Commenter` struct wrapping `MCPCaller` (same interface as `Importer`).

- `func (c *Commenter) PostComment(taskID, comment string) error` — calls `clickup_create_task_comment` MCP tool. Nil-receiver safe (returns nil immediately).

3. Add `buildClickUpProgressComment(event, planName, detail string) string` — formats a concise markdown comment. Prefix with `🤖 kasmos:` so comments are identifiable. Events:

31: 	Topics map[string]jsonTopicEntry `json:"topics"`
32: }

34: // MigrateFromJSON reads plan-state.json from plansDir and imports all plans
35: // and topics into the store under the given project. If plan-state.json does
36: // not exist, it returns (0, nil) — a no-op. The migration is idempotent:
37: // plans and topics that already exist in the store are silently skipped.
38: // For each plan entry that has a corresponding .md file in plansDir, the file
39: // content is also imported via SetContent.
40: //
41: // Returns the number of plans successfully migrated (newly created).
42: func MigrateFromJSON(store Store, project, plansDir string) (int, error) {
43: 	stateFile := filepath.Join(plansDir, "plan-state.json")
44: 
45: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(stateFile)
46: 	if err != nil {
47: 		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
48: 			return 0, nil
49: 		}
50: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("read plan-state.json: %w", err)
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	var state jsonPlanState
54: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &state); err != nil {
55: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("parse plan-state.json: %w", err)
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	migrated := 0
59: 
60: 	// Migrate plans.
61: 	for filename, jp := range state.Plans {
62: 		entry := PlanEntry{
63: 			Filename:    filename,
64: 			Status:      Status(jp.Status),
65: 			Description: jp.Description,
66: 			Branch:      jp.Branch,
67: 			Topic:       jp.Topic,
68: 			Implemented: jp.Implemented,
69: 		}
70: 		if jp.CreatedAt != "" {
71: 			entry.CreatedAt = parseTime(jp.CreatedAt)
72: 		}
73: 
74: 		if err := store.Create(project, entry); err != nil {
75: 			// Skip if already exists (idempotent).
76: 			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "plan already exists") {
77: 				continue
78: 			}
79: 			return migrated, fmt.Errorf("migrate plan %s: %w", filename, err)
80: 		}
81: 		migrated++
82: 
83: 		// Import .md file content if it exists on disk.
84: 		mdPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, filename)
85: 		content, err := os.ReadFile(mdPath)
86: 		if err != nil {
87: 			if !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
88: 				return migrated, fmt.Errorf("read plan content %s: %w", filename, err)
89: 			}
90: 			// No .md file — that's fine, content stays empty.
91: 		} else {
92: 			if err := store.SetContent(project, filename, string(content)); err != nil {
93: 				return migrated, fmt.Errorf("set content for %s: %w", filename, err)
94: 			}
95: 		}
96: 	}
97: 
98: 	// Migrate topics.
99: 	for name, jt := range state.Topics {
100: 		var createdAt time.Time
101: 		if jt.CreatedAt != "" {
102: 			createdAt = parseTime(jt.CreatedAt)
103: 		}
104: 		entry := TopicEntry{
105: 			Name:      name,
106: 			CreatedAt: createdAt,
107: 		}
108: 		if err := store.CreateTopic(project, entry); err != nil {
109: 			// Skip if already exists (idempotent).
110: 			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "topic already exists") {
111: 				continue
112: 			}
113: 			return migrated, fmt.Errorf("migrate topic %s: %w", name, err)
114: 		}
115: 	}
116: 
117: 	return migrated, nil
118: }

package planstore

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"time"
)

// jsonPlanEntry is the on-disk format for a single plan in plan-state.json.
type jsonPlanEntry struct {
	Status      string `json:"status"`
	Description string `json:"description,omitempty"`
	Branch      string `json:"branch,omitempty"`
	Topic       string `json:"topic,omitempty"`
	CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
	Implemented string `json:"implemented,omitempty"`

<content>1: package cmd
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"os"
6: 	"path/filepath"
7: 	"strings"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config"
10: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/planfsm"
11: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/planstate"
12: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/planstore"
13: 	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
14: )
15: 
16: // executePlanRegister registers a plan file that exists on disk but isn't
17: // tracked in plan state yet. It extracts a description from the first markdown
18: // heading and uses the conventional branch name format.
19: func executePlanRegister(plansDir, planFile, branch, topic, description string, store planstore.Store) error {
20: 	fullPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, planFile)
21: 	if _, err := os.Stat(fullPath); err != nil {
22: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan file not found on disk: %s", fullPath)
23: 	}
24: 	ps, err := loadPlanState(plansDir, store)
25: 	if err != nil {
26: 		return err
27: 	}
28: 	if description == "" {
29: 		data, err := os.ReadFile(fullPath)
30: 		if err != nil {
31: 			return err
32: 		}
33: 		description = strings.TrimSuffix(planFile, ".md")
34: 		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(data), "\n") {
35: 			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "# ") {
36: 				description = strings.TrimPrefix(line, "# ")
37: 				break
38: 			}
39: 		}
40: 	}
41: 	// Default branch: plan/<slug> where slug strips the date prefix.
42: 	if branch == "" {
43: 		slug := planFile
44: 		if len(slug) > 11 && slug[4] == '-' && slug[7] == '-' && slug[10] == '-' {
45: 			slug = slug[11:]
46: 		}
47: 		slug = strings.TrimSuffix(slug, ".md")
48: 		branch = "plan/" + slug
49: 	}
50: 	info, _ := os.Stat(fullPath)
51: 	createdAt := info.ModTime()
52: 	return ps.Create(planFile, description, branch, topic, createdAt)
53: }
54: 
55: // executePlanList returns a formatted string listing all plans, optionally
56: // filtered by status. Exported for testing without cobra plumbing.
57: func executePlanList(plansDir, statusFilter string, store planstore.Store) string {
58: 	ps, err := loadPlanState(plansDir, store)
59: 	if err != nil {
60: 		return fmt.Sprintf("error: %v", err)
61: 	}
62: 	var sb strings.Builder
63: 	for _, info := range ps.List() {
64: 		if statusFilter != "" && string(info.Status) != statusFilter {
65: 			continue
66: 		}
67: 		line := fmt.Sprintf("%-14s %-50s %s", info.Status, info.Filename, info.Branch)
68: 		sb.WriteString(strings.TrimRight(line, " ") + "\n")
69: 	}
70: 	return sb.String()
71: }
72: 
73: // executePlanListWithStore returns a formatted string listing all plans from a
74: // remote store backend. storeURL is the base URL of the plan store server
75: // (e.g. "http://athena:7433") and project is the project name to query.
76: func executePlanListWithStore(storeURL, project string) string {
77: 	store := planstore.NewHTTPStore(storeURL, project)
78: 	ps, err := planstate.Load(store, project, "")
79: 	if err != nil {
80: 		return fmt.Sprintf("error: %v", err)
81: 	}
82: 	var sb strings.Builder
83: 	for _, info := range ps.List() {
84: 		line := fmt.Sprintf("%-14s %-50s %s", info.Status, info.Filename, info.Branch)
85: 		sb.WriteString(strings.TrimRight(line, " ") + "\n")
86: 	}
87: 	return sb.String()
88: }
89: 
90: // executePlanSetStatus force-overrides a plan's status, bypassing the FSM.
91: // Requires force=true to prevent accidental misuse.
92: func executePlanSetStatus(plansDir, planFile, status string, force bool, store planstore.Store) error {
93: 	if !force {
94: 		return fmt.Errorf("--force required to override plan status (this bypasses the FSM)")
95: 	}
96: 	ps, err := loadPlanState(plansDir, store)
97: 	if err != nil {
98: 		return err
99: 	}
100: 	return ps.ForceSetStatus(planFile, planstate.Status(status))
101: }
102: 
103: // executePlanTransition applies a named FSM event to a plan and returns the new status.
104: func executePlanTransition(plansDir, planFile, event string, store planstore.Store) (string, error) {
105: 	eventMap := map[string]planfsm.Event{
106: 		"plan_start":         planfsm.PlanStart,
107: 		"planner_finished":   planfsm.PlannerFinished,
108: 		"implement_start":    planfsm.ImplementStart,
109: 		"implement_finished": planfsm.ImplementFinished,
110: 		"review_approved":    planfsm.ReviewApproved,
111: 		"review_changes":     planfsm.ReviewChangesRequested,
112: 		"request_review":     planfsm.RequestReview,
113: 		"start_over":         planfsm.StartOver,
114: 		"reimplement":        planfsm.Reimplement,
115: 		"cancel":             planfsm.Cancel,
116: 		"reopen":             planfsm.Reopen,
117: 	}
118: 	fsmEvent, ok := eventMap[event]
119: 	if !ok {
120: 		names := make([]string, 0, len(eventMap))
121: 		for k := range eventMap {
122: 			names = append(names, k)
123: 		}
124: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("unknown event %q; valid events: %s", event, strings.Join(names, ", "))
125: 	}
126: 	fsm := newFSM(plansDir, store)
127: 	if err := fsm.Transition(planFile, fsmEvent); err != nil {
128: 		return "", err
129: 	}
130: 	ps, err := loadPlanState(plansDir, store)
131: 	if err != nil {
132: 		return "", err
133: 	}
134: 	entry, _ := ps.Entry(planFile)
135: 	return string(entry.Status), nil
136: }
137: 
138: // executePlanImplement transitions a plan into implementing state and writes
139: // a wave signal file so the TUI metadata tick can pick it up.
140: func executePlanImplement(plansDir, planFile string, wave int, store planstore.Store) error {
141: 	if wave < 1 {
142: 		return fmt.Errorf("wave number must be >= 1, got %d", wave)
143: 	}
144: 	fsm := newFSM(plansDir, store)
145: 	ps, err := loadPlanState(plansDir, store)
146: 	if err != nil {
147: 		return err
148: 	}
149: 	entry, ok := ps.Entry(planFile)
150: 	if !ok {
151: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", planFile)
152: 	}
153: 	current := planfsm.Status(entry.Status)
154: 	// If still in planning, finish that phase first (→ ready).
155: 	if current == planfsm.StatusPlanning {
156: 		if err := fsm.Transition(planFile, planfsm.PlannerFinished); err != nil {
157: 			return err
158: 		}
159: 	}
160: 	// Advance to implementing unless already there.
161: 	if current != planfsm.StatusImplementing {
162: 		if err := fsm.Transition(planFile, planfsm.ImplementStart); err != nil {
163: 			return err
164: 		}
165: 	}
166: 
167: 	// Write the wave signal file consumed by the TUI metadata tick.
168: 	// plansDir is <repo>/docs/plans — go up two levels to reach the repo root.
169: 	repoRoot := filepath.Dir(filepath.Dir(plansDir))
170: 	signalsDir := filepath.Join(repoRoot, ".kasmos", "signals")
171: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(signalsDir, 0o755); err != nil {
172: 		return err
173: 	}
174: 	signalName := fmt.Sprintf("implement-wave-%d-%s", wave, planFile)
175: 	return os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(signalsDir, signalName), nil, 0o644)
176: }
177: 
178: // NewPlanCmd builds the `kq plan` cobra command tree.
179: func NewPlanCmd() *cobra.Command {
180: 	planCmd := &cobra.Command{
181: 		Use:   "plan",
182: 		Short: "manage plan lifecycle (list, set-status, transition, implement)",
183: 	}
184: 
185: 	// kq plan list
186: 	var statusFilter string
187: 	listCmd := &cobra.Command{
188: 		Use:   "list",
189: 		Short: "list all plans with status",
190: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
191: 			plansDir, err := resolvePlansDir()
192: 			if err != nil {
193: 				return err
194: 			}
195: 			fmt.Print(executePlanList(plansDir, statusFilter, resolveStore(plansDir)))
196: 			return nil
197: 		},
198: 	}
199: 	listCmd.Flags().StringVar(&statusFilter, "status", "", "filter by status (ready, planning, implementing, reviewing, done, cancelled)")
200: 	planCmd.AddCommand(listCmd)
201: 
202: 	// kq plan register
203: 	var branchFlag, topicFlag, descriptionFlag string
204: 	registerCmd := &cobra.Command{
205: 		Use:   "register <plan-file>",
206: 		Short: "register an untracked plan file (sets status to ready)",
207: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
208: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
209: 			plansDir, err := resolvePlansDir()
210: 			if err != nil {
211: 				return err
212: 			}
213: 			if err := executePlanRegister(plansDir, args[0], branchFlag, topicFlag, descriptionFlag, resolveStore(plansDir)); err != nil {
214: 				return err
215: 			}
216: 			fmt.Printf("registered: %s → ready\n", args[0])
217: 			return nil
218: 		},
219: 	}
220: 	registerCmd.Flags().StringVar(&branchFlag, "branch", "", "override branch name (default: plan/<slug>)")
221: 	registerCmd.Flags().StringVar(&topicFlag, "topic", "", "assign plan to a topic group (auto-creates topic if needed)")
222: 	registerCmd.Flags().StringVar(&descriptionFlag, "description", "", "override description (default: extracted from first # heading)")
223: 	planCmd.AddCommand(registerCmd)
224: 
225: 	// kq plan set-status
226: 	var forceFlag bool
227: 	setStatusCmd := &cobra.Command{
228: 		Use:   "set-status <plan-file> <status>",
229: 		Short: "force-override a plan's status (bypasses FSM)",
230: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(2),
231: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
232: 			plansDir, err := resolvePlansDir()
233: 			if err != nil {
234: 				return err
235: 			}
236: 			if err := executePlanSetStatus(plansDir, args[0], args[1], forceFlag, resolveStore(plansDir)); err != nil {
237: 				return err
238: 			}
239: 			fmt.Printf("%s → %s\n", args[0], args[1])
240: 			return nil
241: 		},
242: 	}
243: 	setStatusCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&forceFlag, "force", false, "confirm intent to bypass FSM transition rules")
244: 	planCmd.AddCommand(setStatusCmd)
245: 
246: 	// kq plan transition
247: 	transitionCmd := &cobra.Command{
248: 		Use:   "transition <plan-file> <event>",
249: 		Short: "apply an FSM event to a plan",
250: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(2),
251: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
252: 			plansDir, err := resolvePlansDir()
253: 			if err != nil {
254: 				return err
255: 			}
256: 			newStatus, err := executePlanTransition(plansDir, args[0], args[1], resolveStore(plansDir))
257: 			if err != nil {
258: 				return err
259: 			}
260: 			fmt.Printf("%s → %s\n", args[0], newStatus)
261: 			return nil
262: 		},
263: 	}
264: 	planCmd.AddCommand(transitionCmd)
265: 
266: 	// kq plan implement
267: 	var waveNum int
268: 	implementCmd := &cobra.Command{
269: 		Use:   "implement <plan-file>",
270: 		Short: "trigger implementation of a specific wave",
271: 		Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
272: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
273: 			plansDir, err := resolvePlansDir()
274: 			if err != nil {
275: 				return err
276: 			}
277: 			if err := executePlanImplement(plansDir, args[0], waveNum, resolveStore(plansDir)); err != nil {
278: 				return err
279: 			}
280: 			fmt.Printf("implementation triggered: %s wave %d\n", args[0], waveNum)
281: 			return nil
282: 		},
283: 	}
284: 	implementCmd.Flags().IntVar(&waveNum, "wave", 1, "wave number to trigger (default: 1)")
285: 	planCmd.AddCommand(implementCmd)
286: 
287: 	return planCmd
288: }
289: 
290: // resolveStoreConfig returns the remote store and project name from config.
291: // Returns (nil, "") when no remote store is configured.
292: func resolveStoreConfig(plansDir string) (planstore.Store, string) {
293: 	cfg := config.LoadConfig()
294: 	if cfg.PlanStore == "" {
295: 		return nil, ""
296: 	}
297: 	project := projectFromPlansDir(plansDir)
298: 	store, err := planstore.NewStoreFromConfig(cfg.PlanStore, project)
299: 	if err != nil || store == nil {
300: 		return nil, ""
301: 	}
302: 	return store, project
303: }
304: 
305: // projectFromPlansDir extracts the repo/project name from a plansDir path.
306: // plansDir is typically <repo>/docs/plans — go up two levels.
307: func projectFromPlansDir(plansDir string) string {
308: 	return filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(filepath.Dir(plansDir)))
309: }
310: 
311: // localSQLiteStore opens (or creates) the local SQLite plan store at the
312: // default path ($HOME/.config/kasmos/plans.db). Used as a fallback when no
313: // remote store is configured.
314: func localSQLiteStore() (planstore.Store, error) {
315: 	dbPath := os.ExpandEnv("$HOME/.config/kasmos/plans.db")
316: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dbPath), 0o755); err != nil {
317: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create kasmos config dir: %w", err)
318: 	}
319: 	return planstore.NewSQLiteStore(dbPath)
320: }
321: 
322: // loadPlanState loads plan state using the store backend.
323: // When store is nil, falls back to the local SQLite store.
324: func loadPlanState(plansDir string, store planstore.Store) (*planstate.PlanState, error) {
325: 	if store == nil {
326: 		var err error
327: 		store, err = localSQLiteStore()
328: 		if err != nil {
329: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("open local plan store: %w", err)
330: 		}
331: 	}
332: 	return planstate.Load(store, projectFromPlansDir(plansDir), plansDir)
333: }
334: 
335: // newFSM creates a PlanStateMachine backed by the given store.
336: // When store is nil, falls back to the local SQLite store.
337: func newFSM(plansDir string, store planstore.Store) *planfsm.PlanStateMachine {
338: 	if store == nil {
339: 		var err error
340: 		store, err = localSQLiteStore()
341: 		if err != nil {
342: 			// Panic is acceptable here — this is a CLI tool and the store
343: 			// is required for all operations.
344: 			panic("newFSM: open local plan store: " + err.Error())
345: 		}
346: 	}
347: 	project := projectFromPlansDir(plansDir)
348: 	return planfsm.New(store, project, plansDir)
349: }
350: 
351: // resolveStore returns the remote plan store from config, or nil if not
352: // configured or unreachable.
353: func resolveStore(plansDir string) planstore.Store {
354: 	store, _ := resolveStoreConfig(plansDir)
355: 	if store != nil && store.Ping() == nil {
356: 		return store
357: 	}
358: 	return nil
359: }
360: 
361: // resolvePlansDir resolves docs/plans/ relative to cwd, returning an error if absent.
362: func resolvePlansDir() (string, error) {
363: 	cwd, err := os.Getwd()
364: 	if err != nil {
365: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("get cwd: %w", err)
366: 	}
367: 	dir := filepath.Join(cwd, "docs", "plans")
368: 	if _, err := os.Stat(dir); err != nil {
369: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("plans directory not found: %s", dir)
370: 	}
371: 	return dir, nil
372: }

(End of file - total 372 lines)
</content>

14: // HTTPStore is a Store implementation that talks to a remote plan store server
15: // over HTTP. Connection errors are wrapped with "plan store unreachable" so
16: // callers can detect and surface them gracefully.
17: type HTTPStore struct {
18: 	baseURL string
19: 	project string
20: 	client  *http.Client
21: }
22: 
23: // NewHTTPStore creates a new HTTPStore client pointing at baseURL.
24: // project is the default project name used when routing requests.
25: // The underlying http.Client has a 5-second timeout.
26: func NewHTTPStore(baseURL, project string) *HTTPStore {
27: 	return &HTTPStore{
28: 		baseURL: strings.TrimRight(baseURL, "/"),
29: 		project: project,
30: 		client:  &http.Client{Timeout: 5 * time.Second},
31: 	}
32: }
33: 
34: // planURL builds the base URL for a project's plans endpoint.
35: func (s *HTTPStore) planURL(project string) string {
36: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s/v1/projects/%s/plans", s.baseURL, url.PathEscape(project))
37: }
38: 
39: // planItemURL builds the URL for a specific plan entry.
40: func (s *HTTPStore) planItemURL(project, filename string) string {
41: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s/v1/projects/%s/plans/%s", s.baseURL, url.PathEscape(project), url.PathEscape(filename))
42: }
43: 
44: // planContentURL builds the URL for a specific plan's content endpoint.
45: func (s *HTTPStore) planContentURL(project, filename string) string {
46: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s/v1/projects/%s/plans/%s/content", s.baseURL, url.PathEscape(project), url.PathEscape(filename))
47: }
48: 
49: // topicURL builds the base URL for a project's topics endpoint.
50: func (s *HTTPStore) topicURL(project string) string {
51: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s/v1/projects/%s/topics", s.baseURL, url.PathEscape(project))
52: }
53: 
54: // do executes an HTTP request and returns the response body.
55: // It wraps connection errors with "plan store unreachable".
56: func (s *HTTPStore) do(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
57: 	resp, err := s.client.Do(req)
58: 	if err != nil {
59: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("plan store unreachable: %w", err)

19: 			return
20: 		}
21: 		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
22: 	})

25: 	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/projects/{project}/plans", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
26: 		project := r.PathValue("project")
27: 		statusFilters := r.URL.Query()["status"]
28: 		topicFilter := r.URL.Query().Get("topic")
29:

37: 		case len(statusFilters) > 0:

39: 			for i, s := range statusFilters {
40: 				statuses[i] = Status(s)
41: 			}

43: 		default:

45: 		}
